# Code of Conduct

## Our pledge

We want `rfair` to be a respectful, productive project for people working on
FAIR assessment, research software, metadata, and reproducible science. We
commit to making participation in this project free from harassment and
exclusionary behavior.

## Expected behavior

Participants are expected to:

- Be respectful of different backgrounds, experience levels, and viewpoints.
- Keep technical disagreement focused on evidence, reproducible examples, and
  maintainable solutions.
- Accept constructive feedback and correct mistakes when they are identified.
- Credit prior work, data sources, upstream projects, and contributors.
- Avoid derailing issue threads or pull requests with unrelated discussion.

## Unacceptable behavior

Unacceptable behavior includes:

- Harassment, intimidation, stalking, or sustained disruption.
- Discriminatory language or behavior.
- Personal attacks, insults, or dismissive comments directed at a person rather
  than the work.
- Publishing private information without explicit permission.
- Unwanted sexual attention or imagery.
- Bad-faith conduct that intentionally wastes maintainer or contributor time.

## Reporting

If you experience or observe unacceptable behavior, report it privately to the
maintainer at <a.sofimahmudi@gmail.com>. Include links, screenshots, logs, or
other context when available. Do not open a public issue for conduct reports.

Reports will be handled as confidentially as practical. Maintainers may remove
comments, close issues or pull requests, block users, or take other action
needed to protect the project community.

## Attribution

This code of conduct is based on the Contributor Covenant, version 2.1:
<https://www.contributor-covenant.org/version/2/1/code_of_conduct/>.
